# SRC-101 | Bitcoin Universe Protocol Reference

> Human docs: index.html
> Field reference: reference.html
> Network: Bitcoin
> Classification: Bitcoin Stamps name service

## Summary

A Bitcoin Stamps naming protocol designed to create durable, address-linked name records that are kept separate from ordinary BTC holdings.

## State model

Stamped name records indexed under the SRC-101 rules, with protocol-specific activation and carrier requirements.

## Lifecycle

1. Choose name
2. Create stamp record
3. Confirm index
4. Resolve ownership

## Operations

- Register: Creates a name record under the active SRC-101 rules.
- Resolve: Looks up the indexed record and its current address association.
- Transfer: Uses the protocol's supported ownership-transfer path rather than a generic inscription send.
- Index: Validates the record against the active Stamps implementation.

## Safety

1. SRC-101 is an evolving protocol. Do not copy a generic JSON example into a transaction and assume it is valid.
2. Verify activation height, carrier type, and current indexer release before integration.
3. Keep name assets separate from ordinary BTC UTXO workflows to avoid accidental handling or transfer errors.

## Sources

- Stampchain FAQ: https://stampchain.io/faq
- Bitcoin Stamps indexer: https://github.com/stampchain-io/btc_stamps
